title: Customers whose name starts with a prefix (Snowflake STARTSWITH)
prompt: You're writing Snowflake SQL. Using Snowflake's STARTSWITH function, return the customers whose name begins with 'Customer'. Columns: customer_id, name. Order by customer_id.

requires.message: This question is about STARTSWITH — solve it with that, not LIKE.

hints: STARTSWITH(name, 'Customer') is TRUE when name begins with that prefix.
Use it in the WHERE clause to keep only those rows.
